title: Ladder AI: From AI Access to AI Fluency
description: Personal AI product initiative — designed a platform helping professionals bridge the gap between basic AI usage and true AI fluency through workflow automation and structured skill-building.
subtitle: Bridging the gap from AI access to AI fluency — a personal product initiative designed for AI Product Management.
overview: Ladder AI is a personal product initiative born from a critical observation: most professionals have AI access but lack AI fluency. Through user research across office workers, college graduates, and Product Managers, I identified three north star problems — the Prompting Gap, the Workflow Gap, and the Learning Gap — and designed a platform to systematically address them.
objective: Design an AI fluency platform that moves professionals from basic chat-based usage to advanced AI integration, workflow automation, and continuous skill-building — with a focus on role-specific outcomes for Product Managers.

sections.title: The Problem: Access vs. Fluency
sections.text:
Problem Statement:

Most professionals have access to AI tools but remain stuck at basic usage — unable to integrate AI into their workflows, automate tasks, or apply AI fluently in their roles. There is a significant gap between knowing about AI and being able to use it productively and reliably.

sections.title: Research Process
items: Target Audiences: Conducted interviews with office workers, recent college graduates, and working professionals across multiple industries
Methodology: Structured user interviews combined with workflow observation and pain-point mapping
Sample Size: Cross-functional research across multiple professional segments to identify overlapping patterns
Key Finding: 50-70% of users reported overlapping pain points, with Product Managers showing the strongest signal for AI integration needs

sections.title: Pain Points Identified
items: Tool Discovery Friction: Difficulty discovering and adopting new AI tools amid rapidly evolving landscape
Over-Reliance on Single Tools: Users default to one tool (often ChatGPT) without exploring better alternatives for specific tasks
Lack of Contextual Outputs: Generic AI responses that don't account for role, industry, or specific use case
Non-Technical Barriers: Lack of prompting skills and AI literacy creates a self-reinforcing knowledge gap
Low Trust in AI Outputs: Users revert to traditional tools after experiencing AI mistakes — losing momentum
Workflow Disconnect: AI tools exist in isolation, not integrated into daily task flows
Subscription Fatigue: Cost and complexity of managing multiple AI tool subscriptions

sections.title: North Star Problems
items: Prompting Gap: Users struggle to write effective, role-specific prompts. Without prompting fluency, even powerful AI tools produce mediocre outputs — reinforcing the perception that AI 'doesn't work for my role.'

Workflow Gap: Lack of automation and integration into daily tasks. Users treat AI as a separate destination (a chat window) rather than an embedded layer across their work — limiting compounding value.
Learning Gap: No structured way to improve AI skills or share workflows. Learning happens through random tutorials, social media, and trial-and-error — slow, inefficient, and easy to abandon.

sections.title: Solution: The Ladder AI Platform
sections.text:
Solution Overview:

A guided, gamified AI fluency platform built on four pillars — personalized onboarding, workflow capture, structured teaching, and progress tracking — designed to move professionals from basic Q&A to building and deploying AI-powered systems in their roles.

items.title: Personalized Onboarding
items.text: Skill diagnosis quiz to assess current AI fluency level and identify role-specific needs

items.title: Workflow Capture
items.text: Map daily tasks and identify automation opportunities — turn manual work into AI-augmented workflows

items.title: Structured Teaching
items.text: Lessons with active recall and open-source concepts to build durable prompting and workflow skills

items.title: Rewards & Measurement
items.text: Streaks, progress tracking, and peer-led sessions to encourage ongoing learning and prevent drop-off

items.title: Role-Specific Templates
items.text: Pre-built prompt libraries and workflow templates tailored for Product Managers, marketers, and analysts

items.title: Continuous Application
items.text: Guided practice that moves users from basic prompts to building and deploying AI-powered systems

sections.title: Example: Product Manager Journey
texts: Step 1 — Onboarding: A PM completes an onboarding quiz that assesses their current AI skills, identifies their role's unique workflows (research synthesis, PRD writing, competitive analysis), and benchmarks them against AI-fluent PMs.

Step 2 — Workflow Mapping: The platform helps map daily PM tasks (user interviews, roadmap updates, stakeholder reports) and surfaces the top 3 repetitive workflows ripe for AI augmentation or full automation.

Step 3 — Structured Lessons: The PM follows guided lessons on writing effective prompts (e.g., 'Synthesize 10 user interviews into a 1-page insight report'), building n8n automations, and chaining tools together.

Step 4 — Progress Tracking: Streaks, level-ups, and peer-led sessions keep the PM engaged — moving them from basic AI usage to advanced fluency, with measurable outputs (time saved, workflows automated, prompts refined).

sections.title: Why This Matters for AI Product Management
items: Ladder AI demonstrates end-to-end AI product thinking — from user research to north star problem framing to solution design
It addresses a real, validated market gap (50-70% of professionals share these pain points)
It applies modern PM frameworks (north star metrics, segmented personas, prioritized roadmaps) to an AI-native product
It showcases ability to design for AI fluency, not just AI access — the strategic frontier of AI product management

sections.title: Status & Next Steps
items: Current Status: Pitch deck complete, research validated, prototype concept defined
Next Phase: Build interactive prototype using Google AI Studio + Figma
Validation Plan: Test with 10 PMs to refine onboarding quiz and workflow capture flow
Long-Term Vision: Platform that becomes the de facto AI fluency layer for knowledge workers

items.title: AI-Native User Research
items.text: Identified overlapping pain points across professional segments through structured interviews

items.title: North Star Problem Framing
items.text: Distilled diverse pain points into three north star problems (Prompting, Workflow, Learning gaps)

items.title: AI Product Strategy
items.text: Designed a platform that addresses AI fluency — not just access — the strategic frontier

items.title: PRD & Solution Design
items.text: Built end-to-end product requirements covering onboarding, workflow capture, teaching, and rewards

items.title: Behavior Change Design
items.text: Integrated gamification, streaks, and peer learning to prevent drop-off and drive durable habit formation

items.title: Vision Articulation
items.text: Connected micro-features to a macro vision of professional AI fluency as a competitive advantage

sections.title: Key Learnings
items: Access ≠ Fluency: Distributing tools doesn't create capability — structured learning and habit design do
Role-Specificity Wins: Generic AI advice fails; PMs need PM-specific prompts, marketers need marketer-specific workflows
Workflow Beats Features: Users don't need more AI tools — they need fewer tools, better integrated into existing workflows
Trust is Earned Through Wins: Small, reliable AI wins (one good prompt, one automated workflow) build momentum; large, unreliable demos break it
Gamification Sustains Learning: Without streaks, levels, and social accountability, AI skill-building gets abandoned

title: Google Calendar: Product Thinking
description: Explored performance metrics, problem prioritization, and solution design through comprehensive product thinking framework.
subtitle: A comprehensive product thinking exercise exploring performance metrics, problem prioritization, and solution design.
overview: This case study showcases a deep dive into Google Calendar's product strategy, examining how to enhance user experience through data-driven decision making and user-centric design thinking.
objective: Analyze Google Calendar's product landscape and propose strategic improvements through comprehensive product thinking frameworks.

sections.title: Key Focus Areas
items: Performance Metrics: Understanding key KPIs including user engagement, scheduling frequency, and feature adoption rates
Problem Prioritization: Identifying and ranking user pain points such as scheduling conflicts, notification management, and multi-calendar coordination
Product Thinking: Developing a strategic framework for product improvements
Solution Prioritization: Evaluating solution impact, technical feasibility, and user value
Wireframes: Creating visual mockups for proposed features and UI improvements

sections.title: Methodology
items: Competitive analysis of calendar applications
User research and pain point identification
Metrics analysis and KPI tracking
Solution ideation and prioritization
Wireframe design and user flow documentation

sections.title: Key Insights
items: Users struggle with managing multiple calendar sources efficiently
Notification fatigue is a key pain point affecting user satisfaction
Integration with other Google Workspace tools drives adoption
Mobile scheduling experience needs optimization

sections.title: Proposed Solutions
items: Enhanced multi-calendar management interface
Intelligent notification system with user preference controls
Improved mobile scheduling interface with quick-add features
Smart conflict detection and resolution suggestions

title: Google News: User Growth
description: Driving user engagement and growth through market research, problem prioritization, and strategic product roadmapping.
subtitle: Driving user engagement and growth through market research, problem prioritization, and strategic product planning.
overview: This strategic initiative focused on boosting user engagement and acquiring new users on Google News platform through data-driven insights and targeted feature development.
objective: Identify key growth opportunities and develop a roadmap to increase user acquisition, retention, and engagement on Google News.

sections.title: Key Challenges Identified
items: Low retention rates among casual news readers
Lack of personalization in news feed recommendations
Competitive pressure from other news aggregation platforms
Mobile user experience friction points
Declining trust in news sources among younger demographics

sections.title: Research & Market Analysis
items: Market Trends: Growing preference for personalized news feeds and mobile-first experiences
User Behavior: Users spend average 8-12 minutes per session; drop-off occurs after 3 articles
Competitor Analysis: Key competitors focusing on AI-driven recommendations and community features
Demographic Insights: 45% of users are aged 18-34; mobile accounts for 72% of traffic

sections.title: Problem Prioritization Framework
items: Priority 1 - High Impact: Improve personalization algorithm to increase session duration and article engagement
Priority 2 - High Impact: Optimize mobile onboarding flow to reduce churn within first week
Priority 3 - Medium Impact: Implement push notification strategy for daily active user growth

sections.title: Strategic Product Roadmap
items: Q1: Enhance recommendation algorithm; A/B test personalization features
Q2: Launch topic-based subscriptions; implement save-for-later functionality
Q3: Introduce social sharing features; expand international coverage
Q4: Launch premium features; implement smart digest emails

items.title: User Acquisition
items.text: +25% new user growth YoY

items.title: Retention
items.text: Improve 30-day retention from 35% to 50%

items.title: Engagement
items.text: Increase average session time from 10 to 15 minutes

items.title: Daily Active Users
items.text: +30% DAU growth

title: Groww: Cut Abandonment Levels
description: Reduced user abandonment through competitive research, user research, and hypothesis-driven testing.
subtitle: Reducing user abandonment in the investment app through user research, competitive analysis, and hypothesis-driven testing.
overview: This product initiative tackled a critical business challenge: reducing user abandonment in the investment onboarding and account setup flows. The project combined rigorous user research with competitive benchmarking to identify friction points and design solutions.
objective: Reduce user abandonment rate by 40% through improved onboarding experience and feature accessibility.

sections.title: Problem Statement
sections.text:
Groww was experiencing high abandonment rates during:
  • Account verification and KYC (Know Your Customer) process - 35% abandonment
  • First investment flow - 28% abandonment
  • Fund selection and portfolio setup - 22% abandonment
  • Payment integration - 18% abandonment

sections.title: Research Methodology
texts:
User Research Approach:
  • Conducted 25+ user interviews with abandoned and active users
  • Analyzed user behavior through heatmaps and session recordings
  • Surveyed 500+ users on friction points and pain areas
  • Tracked drop-off patterns across mobile and web platforms

Competitive Research:
  • Analyzed onboarding flows of 8 competing fintech platforms
  • Benchmarked conversion rates and abandonment metrics
  • Identified best practices in KYC and verification
  • Evaluated UX patterns for payment integration

items.title: KYC Complexity
items.text: Users found the verification process confusing with unclear instructions. Average time: 12 minutes. Users unfamiliar with financial terminology abandoned at 2x rate.

items.title: Choice Paralysis
items.text: Overwhelming fund options without proper guidance led to decision fatigue. Users with more than 20 fund recommendations abandoned.

items.title: Trust & Education Gap
items.text: First-time investors needed more education on investment concepts before committing funds.

items.title: Mobile Friction
items.text: 60% of abandonment occurred on mobile due to small form fields and difficult document uploads.

sections.title: Hypotheses & Testing
items:
Hypothesis #1: Simplifying the KYC flow with step-by-step guidance will increase completion rate by 25%

Test: A/B tested streamlined 3-step KYC vs. previous 6-step flow
Result: ✅ 32% improvement in completion rate

Hypothesis #2: Curated fund recommendations (top 5) vs. all funds will reduce abandonment in portfolio selection

Test: Tested curated recommendations with AI-based personalization
Result: ✅ 28% reduction in abandonment, 40% increase in conversion

Hypothesis #3: Educational tooltips and micro-learning modules will improve completion in first investment flow

Test: Added contextual education features and glossary
Result: ✅ 22% improvement in completion, higher user confidence

sections.title: Solutions Implemented
items: Simplified KYC: Redesigned 6-step process into 3 clear steps with progress indicator
Smart Fund Recommendations: AI-powered curation showing top 5 recommended funds based on investment profile
In-app Education: Tooltips, video tutorials, and glossary for financial terms
Mobile Optimization: Larger form fields, improved document upload with camera functionality
Progress Saving: Allow users to save progress and resume later without re-entering data
Security Reassurance: Clear communication of security measures and data privacy

items.title: Overall Abandonment Reduction
items.text: 40% decrease in overall abandonment rate

items.title: Conversion Increase
items.text: 35% increase in completed first investments

items.title: Time Reduction
items.text: Average onboarding time reduced from 18 to 8 minutes

items.title: User Satisfaction
items.text: NPS improved from 32 to 54 points

sections.title: View Full Case Study
sections.label: View Full Case Study Presentation →
sections.note: Note: The presentation includes detailed user research transcripts, competitive analysis, A/B test results, and wireframes.

sections.title: Key Learnings
items: Simplicity beats features - removing 50% of steps resulted in better outcomes
User education is critical for financial products - context matters
Mobile-first design is essential for fintech apps
Hypothesis-driven testing validates assumptions before full implementation
Understanding user psychology (choice paralysis, trust) drives better product decisions

title: Strava: Identify Critical Funnels
description: Identified and optimized critical user funnels using product analytics, competitive research, and funnel analysis.
subtitle: Analyzing key user funnels to identify growth opportunities for both athletes and business partners on the Strava platform.
overview: This analytical deep-dive identified and optimized critical user conversion funnels at Strava, focusing on understanding the customer journey for both individual athletes and business partners (brands, gyms, races). The goal was to identify key value drivers that unlock business and customer success.
objective: Identify bottlenecks in critical conversion funnels and develop targeted improvement strategies to increase user engagement, retention, and monetization opportunities.

items.title: Athlete Signup & Activation
items.flow: Landing → App Download → Account Creation → First Activity Upload → Profile Completion

items.title: Activity Engagement & Social Interaction
items.flow: Record Activity → View Activity → Give Kudos → Follow Friends → Join Segment

items.title: Premium Subscription Path
items.flow: Free User → Discover Premium → Trial Signup → Paid Conversion → Retention

items.title: Business Partner Activation
items.flow: Discover API → API Signup → Integration → Campaign Creation → Active Campaign

sections.title: Data Analysis Methodology
items: Funnel Analysis: Tracked conversion rates and drop-off points across user journeys
Cohort Analysis: Analyzed user behavior by signup date, geography, and device type
Segment Analysis: Compared performance across casual vs. serious athletes
Competitive Benchmarking: Compared metrics against Fitbit, MapMyRun, and other fitness platforms
User Behavior Analysis: Session analytics, feature adoption, and engagement patterns

items.title: Athlete Signup to Active
items.text: Improve from 10% to 18% (80% increase)

items.title: Free-to-Paid Conversion
items.text: Improve from 8% to 15% (87% increase)

items.title: Partner Activation
items.text: Improve from 15% to 28% (87% increase)

items.title: Overall ARR Impact
items.text: Estimated +$15M-$25M annual revenue opportunity

title: Boostcamp: Auto Progression Feature
description: Comprehensive product management case study showcasing end-to-end product thinking, research, strategy, and execution.
subtitle: Solving a critical P0 problem by designing and prototyping an intelligent progression system to improve user retention and unlock the core user journey.
overview: This case study showcases my end-to-end product management approach to solving a critical user retention challenge at Boostcamp. Through user research, strategic PRD writing, and rapid prototyping using Google AI Studio, I identified and solved a fundamental flaw in Boostcamp's progression system that was alienating its most engaged users.
objective: Design and build a working prototype of an intelligent auto-progression feature that dynamically adjusts difficulty based on actual performance metrics.

sections.title: The Problem: P0 - No Auto Progression
sections.text:
Problem Statement:

By failing to adjust target weights based on actual performance, Boostcamp's rigid, linear progression disrupts the core user journey and alienates its most engaged users.

items.title: User Retention
items.text: Improve 30-day retention from 65% to 80% (+23% improvement)

items.title: Session Duration
items.text: Increase average session time from 15 to 19 minutes (+25%)

items.title: Course Completion
items.text: Increase learners completing 10+ lessons from 35% to 60% (+71%)

title: Spotify: Event Booking Experience
description: Identified a broken event booking journey through user research and friction analysis, prioritized solutions using a RICE framework.
subtitle: Streamlining a broken event discovery and ticketing journey through user research, friction analysis, and RICE-prioritized solutions to improve user retention by 10%.
overview: This case study showcases my end-to-end product management approach to solving a critical user engagement challenge at Spotify. Through user research, friction analysis, and strategic prioritization, I identified and addressed key barriers in the event booking journey that were preventing users from discovering and attending live events.
objective: Identify friction points through user research, prioritize opportunities using RICE framework, and design a streamlined ticketing experience that reduces abandonment and improves user retention.

sections.title: The Problem: Broken Event Booking Journey
sections.text:
Problem Statement:

Spotify's event discovery and booking experience creates unnecessary friction at multiple touchpoints, causing users to abandon the journey and miss opportunities to deepen their engagement with artists and live content.

items.title: User Retention
items.text: Improve 12-month retention by 10% through increased event engagement

items.title: Booking Conversion

items.title: Time-to-Complete
